description:
Split `inspectWorkerRuntime` so closed-ticket exited current-branch workers trigger `verifyCurrentBranchWorker` instead of the worktree `autoLandCompletedWorker` path.

## What to implement

In `packages/pi-beadwork-extension/src/orchestrator.ts`:

When `inspectWorkerRuntime` detects a closed-ticket exited worker:
- If `worker.executionMode === "worktree"`: existing `autoLandCompletedWorker` path (unchanged)
- If `worker.executionMode === "current-branch"`: new `verifyCurrentBranchWorker` path

The new function should:
1. Return immediately if worker is already `verified` (idempotency)
2. Use the existing orchestration-lock pattern (promise deduplication per worker) to prevent concurrent verification
3. Build attribution evidence pack (calls .3.2)
4. Run per-worker review (calls .3.3)
5. Apply coordinator triage (calls .3.4)
6. Handle remediation or mark `verified`
7. Route failures to `attention`

## Open-ticket exited workers (before Phase 5)

Until crash recovery is implemented in Phase 5, open-ticket exited workers continue to be handled by existing logic:
- Existing code marks them `failed` (with exit-code-based errors) or leaves them as `attention`
- The bounded loop already surfaces these as operator-attention conditions
- Do NOT try to handle crash recovery here — just let the existing attention mechanism work
- Phase 5 will add smarter judgment (crash vs deliberate block) and auto-reassignment

## Design Rules

- Mirror the existing `autoLandCompletedWorker` pattern structurally but without rebase/merge-back/cleanup
- Reuse the existing orchestration-lock pattern from `inspectWorkerRuntime` (promise deduplication)
- Must be idempotent: safe to call repeatedly from supervisor polls and restarts
- Skip workers already in `verified`, `attention`, or `failed` states
- Only orchestrate for CLOSED ticket workers; open-ticket workers fall through to existing handling
- The skeleton function should accept the full verification pipeline (steps 3-7) as composable operations so later tasks (.3.2, .3.3, .3.4) can implement them incrementally

## Tests

- Current-branch exited workers trigger `verifyCurrentBranchWorker`, not `autoLandCompletedWorker`
- Worktree exited workers still trigger `autoLandCompletedWorker`
- Already-verified workers return immediately
- Concurrent calls to the same worker deduplicate via lock
- Open-ticket exited workers do NOT trigger verification (fall through to existing `attention`/`failed` handling)
- Workers in `attention` or `failed` are not re-orchestrated

## Definition of Done

- Mode branching works correctly in `inspectWorkerRuntime`
- `verifyCurrentBranchWorker` skeleton exists and is callable
- Idempotency and lock deduplication proven by tests
- Open-ticket workers handled gracefully without crash recovery
- lint/test/typecheck pass clean

comments.text: Implemented in commit 0a24d11. Closed current-branch exited workers now route through verifyCurrentBranchWorker skeleton with lock dedupe/idempotency and no worktree landing operations; worktree landing path remains unchanged. Added tests for mode branching, idempotency, dedupe, open-ticket fallthrough, attention/failed skip, and worktree landing behavior. Package lint/test/typecheck pass.

title: Split inspectWorkerRuntime post-exit orchestration by execution mode
